annotations.label: Yang–Mills theory

anchor.snippet: Yang–Mills theory is a quantum field theory for nuclear binding

annotations.note: Quantum field theory and gauge theory as physical theories are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noether's theorem

anchor.snippet: every conserved physical quantity has a matching symmetry

annotations.note: The physics Noether theorem linking symmetries to conserved currents is absent (Mathlib's 'Noetherian' is unrelated ring theory).

annotations.label: Gauge group and charge conservation

anchor.snippet: in electromagnetic fields that matched the conservation of electric charge

annotations.note: Gauge invariance and charge conservation in electrodynamics are not formalized.

annotations.label: Electroweak and QCD gauge groups

anchor.section: Yang and Mills find the nuclear force gauge theory
anchor.snippet: while QCD is an SU(3) Yang–Mills theory

annotations.note: This identification of QCD/electroweak gauge groups is a physics claim and is not formalized.

annotations.label: Standard Model symmetry group

anchor.section: Yang and Mills find the nuclear force gauge theory
anchor.snippet: The Standard Model combines the strong interaction with the unified electroweak interaction

annotations.note: The Standard Model gauge group structure is physics content not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Yang–Mills Lagrangian

anchor.snippet: special examples of gauge theories with a non-abelian symmetry group given by the Lagrangian

annotations.note: The Yang–Mills Lagrangian density is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Field strength tensor

anchor.snippet: the curvature or field-strength form

annotations.note: Mathlib has covariant derivatives but no curvature/field-strength form for connections.

annotations.label: Structure constants

anchor.snippet: are structure constants of the Lie algebra

annotations.note: Lie algebras and bases are formalized and structure constants are the bracket coefficients, but no named structure-constant object exists.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative is defined as

annotations.note: Mathlib defines a general covariant derivative (Koszul connection) on a vector bundle, generalizing the gauge covariant derivative but without the specific Yang–Mills connection form.

annotations.label: Coupling constant

anchor.snippet: and g is the coupling constant

annotations.note: The gauge coupling constant is a physics notion not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coupling is a pure number in four dimensions

anchor.snippet: the coupling constant g is a pure number

annotations.note: Dimensional analysis of the coupling constant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Field strength from the commutator

anchor.snippet: can be derived by the commutator

annotations.note: The commutator expression for the gauge field strength is not formalized (no field strength in Mathlib).

annotations.label: Self-interaction and semilinear equations

anchor.snippet: The field has the property of being self-interacting

annotations.note: The nonlinear self-interaction of Yang–Mills fields is not formalized.

annotations.label: Index raising convention

anchor.snippet: whereas for μ and ν it is nontrivial

annotations.note: Raising/lowering spacetime indices with a metric in this tensor convention is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equations of motion

anchor.snippet: From the given Lagrangian one can derive the equations of motion

annotations.note: The Yang–Mills equations of motion are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bianchi identity equals Jacobi identity

anchor.snippet: A Bianchi identity holds

annotations.note: The Jacobi identity for Lie brackets is formalized as lie_jacobi, but the Yang–Mills Bianchi identity for the field strength is not.

annotations.label: Dual field strength tensor

anchor.snippet: Define the dual strength tensor

annotations.note: The Hodge-dual field strength tensor is not formalized (no Hodge star or field strength in Mathlib).

annotations.label: Source term in equations of motion

anchor.snippet: enters into the equations of motion as

annotations.note: The matter current source term in the Yang–Mills equations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauge covariance of currents

anchor.snippet: the currents must properly change under gauge group transformations

annotations.note: Gauge transformation behavior of currents is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-renormalizability above four dimensions

anchor.snippet: Yang–Mills theory is not renormalizable for dimensions greater than four

annotations.note: Renormalizability of quantum field theories is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classical scale invariance in four dimensions

anchor.snippet: these theories share the scale invariance at the classical level

annotations.note: Classical scale invariance of the Yang–Mills action is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generating functional

anchor.snippet: One introduces a generating functional for n -point functions as

annotations.note: The path-integral generating functional is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Faddeev–Popov ghost field

anchor.snippet: with the introduction of a ghost field

annotations.note: Faddeev–Popov ghost fields are not formalized.

annotations.label: Renormalizability and finite corrections

anchor.snippet: The theory is renormalizable and corrections are finite at any order of perturbation theory

annotations.note: Perturbative renormalizability of Yang–Mills theory is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ghost decoupling in QED

anchor.snippet: the ghost field decouples because the gauge group is abelian

annotations.note: Ghost decoupling in abelian gauge theory is not formalized.

annotations.label: Asymptotic freedom

anchor.snippet: One of the most important results obtained for Yang–Mills theory is asymptotic freedom

annotations.note: Asymptotic freedom (running of the coupling) is not formalized.

annotations.label: 't Hooft renormalization

anchor.snippet: worked out their renormalization

annotations.note: The renormalization of non-abelian gauge theories is not formalized.

annotations.label: Renormalizability with massive gauge bosons

anchor.snippet: Renormalizability is obtained even if the gauge bosons described by this theory are massive

annotations.note: Renormalizability with massive gauge bosons (Higgs mechanism) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Donaldson invariants

anchor.snippet: invariants of differentiable structures on four-dimensional manifolds

annotations.note: Donaldson invariants of 4-manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mass gap conjecture

anchor.snippet: the lowest excitations of a pure Yang–Mills theory

annotations.note: The Yang–Mills mass gap conjecture is not formalized.

annotations.label: Confinement conjecture

anchor.snippet: a proof of the confinement property in the presence of additional fermions

annotations.note: The confinement conjecture is not formalized.
